title: When do you pick hosted APIs over open-source models?
prompt:
When do you pick hosted APIs over open-source models?

*Answer in the editor as you would out loud in an interview (a few sentences to a short paragraph is fine). Then use 💡 Hint, 🧠 AI feedback, or 📗 Show answer.*
reference_solution:
APIs when: you need best-available quality, have no GPU infrastructure, need to move fast (no model management), or your volume is low enough that per-token pricing is cheaper than running servers. Open-source when: data privacy requirements (GDPR, healthcare), cost predictability at scale (above ~1M requests/month self-hosting usually wins), fine-tuning needs, or local development.

**Takeaway:** Use both: open-source for dev and privacy, APIs for production quality. Document the tradeoffs.
hint_notes: Use both: open-source for dev and privacy, APIs for production quality. Document the tradeoffs.
